title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Haemul Pajeon Recipe: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Haemul Pajeon with our authentic Korean seafood pancake recipe! Get crispy edges, tender seafood, and a savory dipping sauce. Perfect for any meal.
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon (Korean Seafood Pancake) Recipe
h2Headings: How to Make Haemul Pajeon: Step-by-Step Guide
Tips for the Perfect Crispy Haemul Pajeon
seo.imageAlt: A plate of freshly made Haemul Pajeon, a crispy Korean seafood pancake, garnished with green onions and served with dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Our Haemul Pajeon recipe delivers a perfectly crispy, savory Korean seafood pancake that's bursting with flavor.

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, crispy on the outside and tender within, packed with a delightful medley of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. This iconic dish offers a perfect balance of oceanic flavors and a satisfying chewiness, making it an irresistible appetizer or light meal. Enjoy the authentic taste of Korea with every savory bite of this delicious seafood pancake.

ingredients.notes: shrimp, squid rings, mussels, scallops, cleaned and chopped into bite-sized pieces

instructions.title: Prepare the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, toasted sesame seeds, minced garlic, and red chili flakes (if using). Set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Ensure your mixed seafood is cleaned and chopped into bite-sized pieces. Pat them dry thoroughly with paper towels. Cut the green onions into 3-inch lengths and thinly slice the yellow onion. If using, slice the red chili diagonally.

instructions.title: Make the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, rice flour, cornstarch, salt, and white pepper. Gradually whisk in the cold water until a smooth batter forms. Add the egg and whisk until just combined. The batter should be slightly thick but pourable; adjust with a tiny bit more water if too thick.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients
instructions.text: Gently fold the prepared mixed seafood, green onions, and sliced yellow onion into the batter. Do not overmix. You want the ingredients to be coated but not completely submerged in a thick layer of batter.

instructions.title: Cook the First Pancake
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Once hot, pour about one-quarter of the batter mixture into the pan, spreading it evenly to form a thin, round pancake. Arrange a few extra green onion pieces or red chili slices on top if desired.

instructions.title: Achieve Golden Crispiness
instructions.text: Cook for 3-4 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy, and the edges begin to set. Drizzle another tablespoon of oil around the edges of the pancake, then carefully flip it. Cook for another 3-4 minutes on the second side until golden brown and cooked through. Repeat with the remaining batter, adding more oil as needed for each pancake.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board and slice into wedges. Serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce on the side. For best results, enjoy while still hot and crispy.

tips: For an extra crispy Pajeon, use very cold water in your batter and add a touch of rice flour or cornstarch. Dont make the batter too thick; a thin, even layer promotes crispiness.

Ensure your seafood is patted very dry before adding to the batter to prevent the pancake from becoming soggy. You can use any combination of seafood you like, such as shrimp, squid, mussels, or oysters.

Serve Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking to enjoy its optimal crispiness. Pair it with a simple soy-based dipping sauce for a balanced flavor profile, and consider garnishing with extra fresh green onions or sesame seeds.
article: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake is a beloved Korean Seafood dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
